title: Response Bytes, Content Codings, Character Encodings, and Source Identity · Behavior Case: Charset Decision
summary: The parser receives truncated compressed bytes, guesses the platform encoding, ignores a conflicting declaration, and hashes normalized text as if it were the source. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the charset decision increment in byte-to-Unicode admission ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chang…
objectives: Record transport declarations, byte-order marks, HTML declarations, parser detection, replacement behavior, and the final chosen encoding.
Validate and apply declared content codings in order while distinguishing unsupported, corrupt, partial, and over-budget representations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-bytes-encoding-admission-5
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-bytes-encoding-admission-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def worked_scraper_bytes_encoding_admission_5(wire_bytes: int = 48000, expanded_bytes: int = 120000, byte_budget: int = 250000, decoded_pages: int = 6, visible_decode_failures: int = 2) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorizationworked5",
        "url9036",
        "response5dekrow",
        "treeworked59036",
        "frontier9036worked5",
        "report5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if wire_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < wire_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "byte-accounting"
    if expanded_bytes > byte_budget:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "expansion-budget"
    if decoded_pages < 1:
        return False, decoded_pages, "decode-coverage"
    if visible_decode_failures < 1:
        return False, visible_decode_failures, "decode-evidence"
    assert expanded_bytes <= byte_budget
    return True, expanded_bytes, "bounded-decoding-ledger"

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Charset Decision, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the charset decision defect evidence boundary.

For repair the charset decision defect, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Charset Decision model · Theory Charset Decision
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Charset Decision: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 9218 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile compressed and expanded bytes, character decisions, non-HTML rejection, and source identity; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record transport declarations, byte-order marks, HTML declarations, parser detection, replacement behavior, and the final chosen encoding.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Charset Decision model · Theory Charset Decision.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Charset Decision model · Theory Charset Decision, keep Charset Decision connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record transport declarations, byte-order marks, HTML declarations, parser detection, replacement behavior, and the final chosen encoding.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Charset Decision model · Theory Charset Decision, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Charset Decision precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Charset Decision model · Theory Charset Decision, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 9218 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile compressed and expanded bytes, character decisions, non-HTML rejection, and source identity; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record transport declarations, byte-order marks, HTML declarations, parser detection, replacement behavior, and the final chosen encoding.

For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Charset Decision model · Theory Charset Decision, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: Calling response.text makes character decoding objectively correct.
